symptom: Activity logs not appearing in Microsoft Sentinel after configuring diagnostic settings
rootCause: Diagnostic settings misconfigured — wrong Log Analytics workspace selected or settings not saved
solution: Navigate to Azure Monitor > Diagnostic Settings, verify the correct Log Analytics workspace is selected under Destination details, save settings, then validate with query: AzureActivity | where SubscriptionId contains '<SubId>'. Also validate Sentinel connector settings.

symptom: PowerShell/Azure CLI commands fail when configuring activity log export behind a proxy
rootCause: Missing proxy certificates — proxy intercepts HTTPS traffic but the required CA certificates are not in the Trusted Root store
solution: 1) Verify proxy settings in Network & Internet; 2) Import proxy certificates into Trusted Root Certification Authorities via certmgr.msc; 3) Test with Get-AzActivityLog -MaxRecord 5; 4) Run 'az upgrade' and 'az configure' to ensure CLI is current.

symptom: Permission errors when exporting audit logs to another tenant via Azure Lighthouse
rootCause: User lacks required permissions on target workspace or has incorrect cross-tenant role assignment
solution: 1) Check user role assignments in IAM — assign Log Analytics Contributor or Reader on target workspace; 2) If guest user, reset invitation status; 3) Verify ARM template RoleDefinitionId includes correct permissions; 4) Test configuration and monitor activity logs for errors.

symptom: Activity log CSV download fails with 'file not found' or 'CSV file not prepared' error
rootCause: Excessive log volume requested — the system cannot process a large number of logs in a single download request
solution: 1) Reduce the time range to decrease log volume; 2) Break download into smaller batches; 3) Clear browser cache; 4) Verify the storage account is reachable and correctly configured.

symptom: Authorization Failed error when configuring Activity Log diagnostic settings export to Log Analytics
rootCause: Custom role missing Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings/write permission, or scope set to resource group instead of subscription level
solution: 1) Duplicate Log Analytics Contributor role; 2) Ensure Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings/write permission is included; 3) Set scope to subscription level; 4) If access recently granted, refresh credentials.

symptom: Activity log export to Event Hub in another subscription fails due to permission issues
rootCause: Event Hub shared access policy missing Manage/Send/Listen permissions, or Azure system-assigned managed identity lacks Azure Event Hubs Data Sender role
solution: 1) Check Event Hub namespace shared access policy — enable Manage, Send, Listen; 2) Verify Azure Event Hubs Data Sender role on managed identity via 'az role assignment list --scope <EventHubNamespaceResourceID>'; 3) Check for Event Hub throttling in metrics blade.

symptom: No telemetry data in workspace-based Application Insights after enabling AKS autoinstrumentation
rootCause: Deployment not instrumented - missing monitor.azure.com/instrumentation annotation on deployment or replica set, or network connectivity issues blocking ingestion endpoint from pod
solution: 1) Verify monitor.azure.com/instrumentation annotation on deployment and replica set; 2) If missing, ensure Instrumentation CR is in correct namespace with correct connection string and platform, then restart deployment; 3) Check /var/log/applicationinsights for network errors; 4) kubectl exec into pod and test connectivity to endpoint.

symptom: OpenTelemetry .NET Azure Monitor Exporter: operation name missing from dependency telemetry, device model missing from request/dependency telemetry
rootCause: Known limitations in Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporters for .NET
solution: Known issue, no fix available. Missing operation name causes failures and affects Performance tab. Missing device model affects device cohort analysis.

symptom: Application Insights Java agent JAR file corrupted (about half expected size) when downloaded from browser
rootCause: Browser download mechanism corrupts the JAR file during download
solution: Download using curl or wget instead of browser: curl --location --output applicationinsights-agent-X.Y.Z.jar <github-release-url>

symptom: Warning during Spring Boot native image startup: The OpenTelemetry version is not compatible with spring-cloud-azure-starter-monitor dependency
rootCause: Incompatible OpenTelemetry version with spring-cloud-azure-starter-monitor dependency
solution: Import the OpenTelemetry Bills of Materials (BOM) following the OpenTelemetry Spring Boot starter docs. Enable self-diagnostics via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_SELF_DIAGNOSTICS_LEVEL=DEBUG.

symptom: OpenTelemetry Node.js Azure Monitor Exporter: operation name missing, device model missing, database server name missing from dependency name
rootCause: Known limitations in Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporters for Node.js
solution: Known issues. Database server name omission causes incorrect aggregation of tables with same name on different servers. Enable diagnostic logging via diag.setLogger for troubleshooting.

symptom: Duplicate trace logs in Application Insights when using Azure Functions with OpenTelemetry Python SDK
rootCause: Both native Azure Functions logging and azure-monitor-opentelemetry logging enabled simultaneously
solution: Set OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=None to disable distribution logging. For Always On: set to Off, or shutdown providers between configure_azure_monitor calls.

symptom: Missing Requests table data in Application Insights when using FastAPI or Flask with Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro Python SDK
rootCause: Importing fastapi.FastAPI or flask.Flask before calling configure_azure_monitor() prevents proper HTTP framework instrumentation
solution: Import module as whole (import fastapi), call configure_azure_monitor() before accessing FastAPI class. Correct order: 1) import configure_azure_monitor; 2) import fastapi; 3) configure_azure_monitor(); 4) app = fastapi.FastAPI()

symptom: No telemetry after enabling autoinstrumentation on App Service because Application Insights SDK is already installed in the .NET application
rootCause: Autoinstrumentation detects existing SDK assemblies (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation) and backs off
solution: Remove the Application Insights SDK NuGet packages from the application. Check AppAlreadyInstrumented status at https://{sitename}.scm.azurewebsites.net/ApplicationInsights. Clean bin and wwwroot directories of leftover DLLs.

symptom: ASP.NET Core autoinstrumentation fails silently when application configures ASPNETCORE_HOSTINGSTARTUPASSEMBLIES explicitly
rootCause: Application explicit hosting startup assembly configuration overrides App Service environment settings, excluding Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper
solution: Remove custom hosting startup configuration or explicitly include Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.StartupBootstrapper in the configured hosting startup assemblies list

symptom: Java Azure Functions slow startup time after enabling Application Insights autoinstrumentation
rootCause: Legacy extension settings (XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_Java=1, ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION=~2/~3) from before Feb 2023 cause slow initialization
solution: Remove legacy settings (XDT_MicrosoftApplicationInsights_Java, ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION). Add APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_ENABLE_AGENT=true to enable the latest version.

symptom: Duplicate logs in Application Insights when using Java Azure Functions with log4j or logback console logging
rootCause: Distributed tracing for Java Functions captures console output, creating duplicates when log4j/logback also writes to console
solution: For log4j: add ThresholdFilter with level=ALL onMatch=DENY to Console appender. For logback: add ThresholdFilter with level=OFF to Console appender encoder.

symptom: 500 URL rewrite error on App Service when APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED=true and content encoding (gzip) is active
rootCause: Outbound rewrite rules for JavaScript injection cannot be applied when HTTP response content is encoded with gzip
solution: Remove APPINSIGHTS_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED app setting. Use manual JavaScript SDK references in webpages for client-side monitoring instead.

symptom: System.IO.FileNotFoundException for System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll after App Insights extension upgrade to 2.8.44
rootCause: Extension 2.8.44 upgrades SDK to 2.20.0 which has indirect reference to System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll via DiagnosticSource; binding redirect points to missing assembly
solution: Remove binding redirect for System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe.dll from web.config, or update to oldVersion=0.0.0.0-4.0.4.1 newVersion=4.0.4.1. Temporary workaround: set ApplicationInsightsAgent_EXTENSION_VERSION=2.8.37.

symptom: Duplicate telemetry and unexpected cost increase in Python App Service with Application Insights
rootCause: Both App Service autoinstrumentation and manual OpenTelemetry instrumentation (Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro) enabled simultaneously
solution: Use only one instrumentation method. If using autoinstrumentation, remove manual OpenTelemetry from code. If using manual instrumentation, disable autoinstrumentation.

symptom: High CPU, memory leaks, thread leaks, or TCP port exhaustion in .NET application after enabling Application Insights SDK
rootCause: Application code leaks TelemetryConfiguration objects - each leaked instance creates 2 additional threads. Common causes: creating TelemetryConfiguration per request, or calling services.BuildServiceProvider() in ConfigureServices
solution: For .NET Framework: use TelemetryConfiguration.Active singleton. For .NET Core: use TelemetryConfiguration.CreateDefault() and never call services.BuildServiceProvider() in ConfigureServices.

symptom: FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly Microsoft.AspNet.TelemetryCorrelation after upgrading Application Insights SDK from pre-2.4
rootCause: Missing HTTP modules and telemetry module configuration in web.config and ApplicationInsights.config after SDK upgrade
solution: Add TelemetryCorrelationHttpModule before ApplicationInsightsHttpModule in web.config modules. Add AspNetDiagnosticTelemetryModule in ApplicationInsights.config TelemetryModules.

symptom: JVM fails to start with Error opening zip file or JAR manifest missing when using Application Insights Java agent
rootCause: Agent JAR file not found at specified path or corrupted during file transfer
solution: Verify -javaagent JVM argument points to valid JAR path. Re-download agent JAR using curl/wget instead of browser to avoid corruption.

symptom: Tomcat application takes several minutes to start after enabling Application Insights Java agent
rootCause: Tomcat JAR scanning mechanism scans Application Insights JAR files during startup unnecessarily
solution: Exclude Application Insights JAR files from Tomcat list of scanned files in catalina.properties or context.xml.

symptom: UnknownHostException after upgrading Application Insights Java agent to version later than 3.2.0
rootCause: Java agent versions >3.2.0 use new ingestion endpoint v2.1/track which redirects to nearest regional endpoint; network may not resolve new endpoint
solution: Upgrade network configuration to resolve the new ingestion endpoint shown in the exception. The endpoint automatically redirects to nearest ingestion endpoint for the AI resource.

symptom: Application Insights JavaScript SDK LOAD Failure exception: Failed to load Application Insights SDK script
rootCause: SDK script cannot be downloaded from CDN or fails to initialize. Common causes: intermittent network failure, CDN outage, corporate firewall blocking, invalid SDK configuration
solution: 1) Check network tab in browser DevTools for download status; 2) Verify CDN endpoint js.monitor.azure.com is not blocked; 3) For corporate networks whitelist CDN or use npm packages; 4) Enable loggingLevelConsole:2 for diagnostics; 5) Test minimal config with only instrumentationKey.

symptom: App Insights JS SDK reports 0 duration for page views in SPA
rootCause: By design - SPA route changes do not trigger traditional page load events
solution: Use trackPageView() with manual duration, startTrackPageView()/stopTrackPageView(), or overridePageViewDuration config

symptom: Failed to get Request-Context correlation header error in App Insights JS SDK
rootCause: Third-party server missing correlation headers in Access-Control-Allow-Headers
solution: Add domain to correlationHeaderExcludedDomains or update server CORS headers to include Request-Id, Request-Context, traceparent

symptom: Duplicate telemetry from App Insights JS SDK with connection strings
rootCause: SDK recursive correlation causes duplicate auto-tracked requests to ingestion endpoint
solution: Set excludeRequestFromAutoTrackingPatterns config with endpoint URL

symptom: Third-party server rejects App Insights distributed tracing headers breaking correlation
rootCause: Server Access-Control-Allow-Headers missing Request-Id, traceparent, Request-Context
solution: Add Request-Id, traceparent, Request-Context to server Access-Control-Allow-Headers

symptom: Node.js App Insights SDK not sending telemetry silently
rootCause: SDK misconfiguration or connectivity issues; self-diagnostics not enabled to surface errors
solution: Enable setInternalLogging(true,true); set APPLICATION_INSIGHTS_ENABLE_DEBUG_LOGS env var; check applicationinsights.log in temp dir

symptom: Application Map incomplete - too many nodes exceeding 10K limit
rootCause: Too many unique cloud role names or dependency types/targets exceeding Application Map rendering limits
solution: Set cloud role name properly; use logical dependency targets/types not unique IDs; reduce cardinality

symptom: Application Map Intelligent View does not load or very slow
rootCause: Time frame set to more than 6 days, or Update map components selected, or multiple AI resources enabled
solution: Set time frame to 6 days or less; do not select Update map components; use single AI resource

symptom: Live Metrics pane shows no data or fails to connect
rootCause: Firewall blocking live metrics endpoints (different from standard telemetry) or TLS version older than 1.2
solution: Open live metrics endpoints in firewall; enable TLS 1.2; check ad-blockers not blocking *.livediagnostics.monitor.azure.com

symptom: Missing QuickPulseTelemetryModule causes Live Metrics failure in .NET
rootCause: ApplicationInsights.config missing QuickPulseTelemetryModule and/or QuickPulseTelemetryProcessor entries
solution: Add QuickPulseTelemetryModule to TelemetryModules and QuickPulseTelemetryProcessor to TelemetryProcessors; install PerfCounterCollector NuGet; restart app

symptom: Data is temporarily inaccessible message in Live Metrics pane
rootCause: Azure service outage or ad-blockers/firewall blocking live metrics endpoints
solution: Check Azure status at aka.ms/aistatus; add ad-blocker exclusion for *.livediagnostics.monitor.azure.com

symptom: Low monitored server count in Live Metrics vs actual allocated instances
rootCause: Web servers unload idle applications; Live Metrics only counts servers currently running the app process
solution: Expected behavior. Count increases when traffic resumes and servers reload the application.

symptom: Create work item option missing in App Insights transaction details
rootCause: Work item integration not enabled or resource not linked to Azure DevOps or GitHub
solution: Configure integration in Application Insights > Work Items settings

symptom: Work item creation fails with permission errors in App Insights
rootCause: Missing Contribute (DevOps) or Write (GitHub) permissions, or PAT missing Work Items scope
solution: Assign Contribute for DevOps, Write for GitHub; add Work Items read/write scope to PAT

symptom: Contextual data missing from work items created via App Insights integration
rootCause: KQL query in workbook template returns wrong schema for work item bindings
solution: Run query in Logs to verify output fields; update query/bindings to match expected schema

symptom: Availability test fails with DNS resolution error: The remote name could not be resolved
rootCause: Misconfigured DNS records or temporary DNS server failures
solution: Verify DNS records for the target URL; check for transient DNS outages; ensure the endpoint is publicly resolvable

symptom: Availability test fails at connection establishment: connected party did not properly respond after a period of time
rootCause: Server firewall blocking test agents; server not responding to HTTP requests
solution: Add the Availability service tag to firewall/NSG rules to allow test agent IPs

symptom: Availability test fails at TLS transport: client and server cannot communicate because they do not possess a common algorithm
rootCause: Endpoint uses unsupported TLS version (only TLS 1.2 and 1.3 supported); SSL not supported; some regions have TLS 1.3 restrictions
solution: Ensure endpoint supports TLS 1.2 or 1.3; check TLS 1.3 regional restrictions if issue is region-specific

symptom: Availability test content validation fails: required text did not appear in the response
rootCause: Content match is case-sensitive and exact; fails if response body exceeds 1MB (connection dropped); page content changed
solution: Use exact case-sensitive match string; keep response under 1MB; update match string if page content changed; only English characters supported

symptom: Application Insights availability test intermittently fails with protocol violation error
rootCause: Malformed HTTP response headers not using CRLF line endings; caused by load balancers or CDNs violating HTTP spec
solution: Inspect response headers for CRLF violations; fix server/load balancer/CDN header formatting

symptom: Cannot run Application Insights availability tests on internal server behind firewall
rootCause: Firewall blocks incoming requests from availability test agent IP addresses
solution: Option 1: Whitelist web test agent IPs in firewall. Option 2: Use TrackAvailability API running behind firewall with outgoing access to AI ingestion endpoint

symptom: Application Insights daily cap setting does not limit actual ingested data volume as expected
rootCause: Since March 2023, Application Insights and Log Analytics have separate daily cap implementations; using ikey (global endpoint) makes AI daily cap ineffective across regions
solution: Set daily cap independently for both Application Insights and Log Analytics; use connection strings (regional endpoint) instead of ikey

symptom: Log Analytics workspace receives less telemetry than daily cap allows; daily cap never reached
rootCause: A Data Collection Rule (DCR) filters/reduces telemetry volume before it reaches the workspace
solution: Review and modify or delete the DCR to allow full telemetry volume to reach the Log Analytics workspace

symptom: CPU Total and Committed Memory metrics missing in Application Insights Live Metrics, Performance, and Metrics blades
rootCause: Application pool identity lacks permissions to read computer-level performance counters on Windows
solution: Add the app pool identity to both Performance Log Users and Performance Monitor Users local security groups on the host

symptom: Error retrieving data or Missing localization resource errors on Application Insights portal pages
rootCause: Browser extension interference, network failure, or backend API failure preventing required API calls
solution: Open InPrivate/Incognito window; disable browser extensions; test on other browsers/machines; check DNS/network; capture browser trace if persists

symptom: Application Insights shows only 1 user/session count instead of actual many users/sessions
rootCause: Anonymous user ID and session ID telemetry properties not populated with unique values; JavaScript SDK not added
solution: Add Application Insights JavaScript SDK to all monitored pages; or create telemetry initializer to populate anonymous user ID and session ID

symptom: Application Insights Agent (Status Monitor v2) monitoring fails silently; no telemetry collected
rootCause: Conflicting DLLs in app bin folder (Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.dll, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource.dll) cause Redfield binaries to be skipped
solution: Remove conflicting DLLs from app bin folder; use PerfView/Handle.exe/ListDLLs.exe to verify correct DLLs loaded

symptom: Az.ApplicationMonitor module not working with PowerShell 6 or 7
rootCause: Module only supports PowerShell 5.1; incompatible with PowerShell 6/7
solution: Use PowerShell 5.1 side-by-side with newer PowerShell versions for Application Insights Agent operations

symptom: Application Insights Agent HTTP module not injected when IIS uses shared configuration across web server cluster
rootCause: Enable command installs to local GAC but shared ApplicationHost.config does not reference the module
solution: Run Enable command on each web server; manually add ManagedHttpModuleHelper module entry to shared ApplicationHost.config modules section

symptom: Availability test fails with too many redirects: 'This webpage has too many redirects. This loop will be terminated here since this request exceeded the limit for auto redirects'
rootCause: The target URL performs more than 10 redirects. Application Insights availability tests enforce a limit of 10 redirects per test.
solution: Reduce the redirect chain to 10 or fewer. Use the direct final URL in the availability test configuration instead of the initial redirecting URL.

symptom: Availability test results missing in Azure portal when viewing end-to-end transaction details; no test data displayed
rootCause: The response from the tested endpoint contains non-UTF8 characters. Non-UTF8 characters are not supported for viewing web test results in the Azure portal.
solution: Ensure the endpoint response uses only UTF-8 encoding. Remove or encode any non-UTF8 characters in the response body.

symptom: Availability test fails with 'This URL is not supported' error when testing internal or private IP addresses
rootCause: Availability tests only allow communicating over publicly available IP addresses and hostnames. Internal/private IPs that are not routable via the public internet are not supported.
solution: Use only public IP addresses in web tests. For internal servers behind firewalls, use the TrackAvailability API with custom code running on a test server inside the network, sending results to Application Insights.

symptom: DNS solution deployed to Log Analytics workspace successfully but DNS tables are not populated
rootCause: Table creation is not defined in the ARM template, or Windows DNS Events via AMA Data Connector is not installed/connected (no data ingestion)
solution: 1) Verify ARM template includes table definition; 2) Check Windows DNS Events via AMA Data Connector is installed and connected; 3) If table not auto-created, manually define it in ARM template

symptom: Error 'Cannot read properties of undefined' when creating a custom table in Azure Log Analytics
rootCause: Temporary glitch or misconfiguration in the JSON file used for table creation, or browser cache issues
solution: 1) Validate JSON file format with JSONLint; 2) Check ARM logs in Azure Monitor for errors; 3) Retry table creation using different browser or clear cache; 4) Collect HAR files and console logs for diagnosis

symptom: Error 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading length)' when creating custom log table via JSON file in Azure, occurs at final step despite valid JSON
rootCause: JSON structure issue or DCR configuration conflict — using an existing DCR that collects other data types causes conflicts; also requires at least Contributor role
solution: 1) Validate JSON format; 2) Create a NEW dedicated DCR for custom log table (don't reuse existing DCR); 3) Use PowerShell script per docs to create custom table; 4) Ensure Contributor role on workspace; 5) Refresh portal if issue persists

symptom: File upload errors when creating custom tables in Azure Log Analytics
rootCause: File format not supported by Azure for table creation, or file size exceeds Azure limits
solution: 1) Verify file is in supported format; 2) Check file size doesn't exceed limits, split large files if needed; 3) Upload via Azure portal Custom Logs section

symptom: Performance logs not populating in Azure Government when setting up VM insights alerts using Data Collection Rules (DCR)
rootCause: Alert scope defaults to DCR instead of Log Analytics workspace — DCR cannot access the required data tables directly
solution: Set the alert scope to the Log Analytics workspace associated with the DCR (not the DCR itself), then verify query runs successfully in the workspace

symptom: Unable to query logs in Azure Log Analytics due to log file configuration issues with custom tables
rootCause: Problem with the log file itself (integrity issues), not configuration settings
solution: 1) Verify log file integrity in Log Management section; 2) Ensure application team fixes log file issues; 3) Test log queries in query editor; 4) Monitor log performance with Azure Monitor charts

symptom: Cannot delete migrated custom log tables in Log Analytics
rootCause: Migrated tables may not be deletable through the portal UI; API permissions may be insufficient
solution: Use the Azure Log Analytics 'Tables - Delete' REST API to remove the migrated custom table; verify correct table name and workspace details; ensure necessary API permissions

symptom: MMA agent fails to connect to Log Analytics workspace with error 0x80090016 Keyset does not exist in Event ID 7022/3009
rootCause: Server was cloned from a golden image with MMA already installed and configured; certificates contain the original server name instead of the current hostname
solution: Stop MMA service → open MMC Certificates snap-in → export and delete existing MMA certificates → restart MMA service → new certificates auto-generated with correct hostname

symptom: Linux agent heartbeats missing in Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: Agent configured to report to a different workspace ID than expected
solution: Run `sudo sh /opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/omsadmin.sh -l` to check workspace ID; compare with Azure portal workspace ID; reconfigure agent to point to correct workspace

symptom: Linux agent heartbeats missing — omsagent process not running
rootCause: The omsagent process failed to start or crashed
solution: Check with `ps -ef | grep oms`; if omsagent not running, start manually: `/opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/service_control start`; verify output in omsagent.log

symptom: Linux agent heartbeats missing — SSL connection errors
rootCause: Problems with VM SSL setup: invalid root CA certificates or broken SSL connectivity
solution: Download Ruby SSL test script (`wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ruby/openssl/master/test/openssl/test_ssl.rb`); run as omsagent user; fix CA certificates and SSL connection if test fails

symptom: Linux agent generates heartbeats but 'failed to flush the buffer' / 'Net::OpenTimeout' errors in omsagent.log
rootCause: Network connectivity issues preventing heartbeat transmission to *.ods.opinsights.azure.com endpoint
solution: Run `/opt/microsoft/omsagent/bin/troubleshooter` option 2; verify endpoint connectivity to *.ods.opinsights.azure.com:443, *.oms.opinsights.azure.com:443; consult network team to restore access

symptom: No Linux heartbeats in workspace — KQL query `Heartbeat | where OSType == 'Linux'` returns no results for any agent
rootCause: General Log Analytics ingestion issues or Azure service outage affecting the region
solution: Check Azure service health for outage notifications; verify ingestion latency delays; wait for affected service/region to recover

symptom: Linux performance counters missing in Log Analytics workspace despite heartbeats working
rootCause: Agent not receiving current workspace configuration for performance counter collection; omsagent.conf lacks performance counter source entries
solution: Force config pull: `sudo -u omsagent python /opt/microsoft/omsconfig/Scripts/PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks.py`; or restart agent: `service_control stop` then `start`; verify performance counter entries appear in omsagent.log

symptom: Windows cannot start Microsoft Monitoring Agent service — error code 5 'Access is denied' in System Event Log (Event ID 7024)
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HealthService\Parameters\SecureStorageManager has had default permissions removed (server hardened or script modified ACL), denying Read/Write to SYSTEM and Administrators
solution: Open regedit → navigate to SecureStorageManager key → Edit > Permissions → restore Full Control (Allow) for SYSTEM and Administrators group; back up registry first

symptom: Microsoft Monitoring Agent proxy settings are erased after connecting to SCOM Operations Manager management group — 'Use a proxy server' option cleared
rootCause: Operations Manager specifies a different proxy address that overrides MMA proxy settings; or SCOM Advisor Proxy Setting Rule has an override set to True for the agent
solution: Option A: Disable SCOM proxy server in Azure Log Analytics Settings Wizard then re-enter MMA proxy address; Option B: In SCOM console, delete the override of Advisor Proxy Setting Rule for the agent, then re-enter MMA proxy address

symptom: Deleted Azure Event Hub is automatically re-created by diagnostic settings even after removing the diagnostic setting
rootCause: Diagnostic settings auto-create event hubs when they receive 'not found' errors; diagnostic settings maintain a configuration cache for ~1 hour, so deleted settings continue sending data during that window
solution: Update diagnostic settings to stop sending to the event hub before deleting; if diagnostic settings already deleted, wait at least 1 hour for cache expiry before deleting the event hub

symptom: Cannot fetch role definitions from Azure Log Analytics - errors when retrieving role definitions due to missing subscriptions
rootCause: The required subscription was not added to the Azure account before running role definition retrieval scripts
solution: Verify subscription is active in Azure portal > Subscriptions; run AzureActivity query in Log Analytics workspace to retrieve role definitions; for custom templates, consult a Cloud Solution Architect

symptom: Azure portal notification about legacy log profiles being deprecated; legacy log profiles not visible or errors creating Diagnostic Settings to replace them
rootCause: Azure is phasing out legacy log profiles in favor of Diagnostic Settings by September 2026; legacy profiles may not be visible without proper permissions
solution: 1) In Azure Monitor > Activity Log, identify legacy log profiles; 2) Delete legacy log profiles; 3) Create new Diagnostic Settings under Monitor > Diagnostic Settings; 4) Verify new settings are active and collecting data

symptom: Cannot export activity logs from multiple Azure subscriptions within a management group to Event Hub - configuration limited to one subscription at a time
rootCause: Log export via portal UI is limited to individual subscriptions; management group level diagnostic settings require API-based configuration
solution: Use Management Diagnostic Settings API (not portal) to configure diagnostic settings at the highest-level management group; retrieve management group ID first; verify exported events include the Hierarchy field showing originating management group

symptom: Unexpected charges for Azure Event Hub when using diagnostic settings to export data; confusion about ingress event billing and throughput unit costs
rootCause: Event Hub billing based on ingress events (64 KB units) and throughput units (hourly max); even short-lived data incurs hourly throughput charges
solution: 1) Select appropriate Event Hub SKU (Basic/Standard/Premium/Dedicated) for your data volume; 2) Configure diagnostic settings to filter by category (e.g., Administrative only); 3) Monitor and adjust throughput units; 4) Review pricing at https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/event-hubs

symptom: Cannot export Azure activity logs for a specific resource group - Azure does not support direct log export per resource group
rootCause: Azure activity log export is subscription-level, not resource-group-level; no native filtering at export time
solution: Use Azure Monitor > Activity Logs with Resource column filter to narrow by resource group; or query AzureActivity table in Log Analytics with resource group filter; export filtered results

symptom: Cannot add a 5th diagnostic setting - blocked by a non-existent 'ghost' diagnostic setting that counts toward the limit
rootCause: A legacy log profile exists that counts as a diagnostic setting slot; the ghost setting is actually an old log profile that must be removed
solution: 1) Verify resource has 5 diagnostic settings listed; 2) Use Azure Resource Explorer to check resource status; 3) Delete the legacy log profile via Azure API; 4) Confirm deletion with customer; 5) If issue persists, file IcM for investigation

symptom: Azure activity logs not forwarding after migrating from legacy log profiles to diagnostic settings; automation scripts fail after transition
rootCause: Legacy API (Get-AzLogProfile) still in use; automation scripts not updated to diagnostic settings API; 'default' log profile may still exist but disabled
solution: 1) Run Get-AzLogProfile to identify remaining legacy profiles; 2) Update automation scripts to use diagnostic settings API; 3) Configure diagnostic settings in Azure Monitor > Activity Logs > Diagnostic settings; 4) Monitor logs to verify forwarding works

symptom: Azure Activity Logs need to be retained beyond the default 90-day period for compliance or extended analysis
rootCause: Default Activity Log retention is only 90 days; no long-term storage configured
solution: 1) Create diagnostic setting in Azure Monitor > Diagnostic Settings; 2) Select Azure Storage as destination; 3) Navigate to Storage Account > Data Management > Lifecycle Management and set retention period to desired duration; 4) Verify configuration by checking destination resource for stored logs.

symptom: Activity log export configured but logs not appearing in Log Analytics, Event Hub, or Storage Account destinations
rootCause: Legacy API/PowerShell log profile methods are outdated; diagnostic settings may not be saved; destination resources may have issues (Event Hub throttling, wrong Storage container)
solution: 1) Update legacy log profiles to diagnostic settings; 2) For Log Analytics: run AzureActivity | summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated,1d) to verify ingestion; 3) For Event Hub: check Data Explorer and metrics blade for throttling; 4) For Storage: verify insights-activity-logs container exists; 5) Wait ~30 min for export to begin after new configuration.

symptom: Application Insights telemetry missing in Azure portal even though instrumentation appears correct - Entra ID authentication configured on resource but application not authenticating
rootCause: Application Insights data collection endpoint configured to use Microsoft Entra ID authentication but the application is not configured to authenticate with Entra ID, causing telemetry to be rejected
solution: Configure the application to authenticate using Microsoft Entra ID by following the Enable Microsoft Entra ID authentication documentation. Ensure SDK sends to v2.1/track endpoint (not v2/track which does not support Entra ID).

symptom: HTTP 401 Authorization required error when Application Insights SDK sends telemetry with Microsoft Entra authentication enabled
rootCause: SDK is configured correctly for Entra auth but cannot acquire a valid token - issue with Microsoft Entra ID or Azure Identity configuration
solution: 1) Check SDK logs for 'Failed to get AAD Token' exceptions; 2) For .NET: collect event source logs with PerfView; 3) For Java: inspect network traffic with Fiddler, check for CredentialUnavailableException in logs; 4) Verify managed identity or client secret configuration is correct.

symptom: HTTP 403 Forbidden error when Application Insights SDK sends telemetry - credentials do not grant access to ingest telemetry
rootCause: Credentials used by SDK lack the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role assignment on the Application Insights resource
solution: 1) Verify the identity (system-assigned MI, user-assigned MI, or service principal) has Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the App Insights resource; 2) For user-assigned identity, ensure it is properly associated and role-assigned; 3) For Java: check agent logs for 'Failed to send telemetry with status code: 403'.

symptom: No data appearing in Application Insights portal after adding Java 2.x SDK to web project
rootCause: Instrumentation key not defined or misconfigured in ApplicationInsights.xml; telemetry disabled; firewall blocking ports 80/443; wrong config file location
solution: 1) Verify iKey in ApplicationInsights.xml (resources folder); 2) Check no DisableTelemetry=true node exists; 3) Open TCP ports 80 and 443 for dc.services.visualstudio.com; 4) Add SDKLogger element to enable debug logging; 5) Verify ApplicationInsights.xml is in correct classpath location (e.g., WEB-INF/classes for Tomcat).

symptom: Application Insights Java 2.x was showing data but it has stopped - no new telemetry appearing
rootCause: Monthly data quota exceeded, duplicate SDK versions in classpath after upgrade, or iKey mismatch between app and resource
solution: 1) Check Settings > Quota and Pricing for monthly quota status; 2) After SDK upgrade, verify only one version of SDK JARs exists in project; 3) Confirm iKey matches the correct Application Insights resource; 4) Check Usage and estimated cost page for sampling status.

symptom: Application Insights JavaScript SDK logs 'Failed to get Request-Context correlation header as it may be not included in the response or not accessible' error
rootCause: Third-party domains do not return correlation headers (Request-Id, Request-Context, traceparent), causing the SDK to log errors when attempting cross-origin correlation
solution: Add the third-party domains to the correlationHeaderExcludedDomains configuration property (supports wildcards, e.g., '*.queue.core.windows.net'). Do NOT add your own application domain to this list as it would disable distributed tracing for your app.
